arguments.task: Research Pylon using official first-party pages only. Open and read the strongest canonical pages that support: product description, API/developer docs, authentication methods, pricing/access tier, and any MCP/server documentation. Avoid search-result snippets. Return a concise evidence-backed summary with the exact URLs visited and what each page establishes. If the site is JS-rendered or docs are thin/gated, explicitly say so. Also check whether Pylon exposes REST or GraphQL APIs from official docs, and whether there is an official or clearly documented MCP server/integration.

input.thought: Use the browser to inspect official Pylon pages and extract evidence-backed API, auth, pricing, and MCP details.

raw_result.description: B2B customer support platform that centralizes conversations and issue tracking across channels like Slack, email, Teams, and in-app chat.
raw_result.auth_notes: Pylon's public REST API docs specify Bearer token auth via an API token generated by an admin in the Pylon dashboard. Pylon's official MCP server docs specify OAuth 2.0 authentication via AuthKit for connecting AI clients to the MCP server.
raw_result.gating_reason: The official pricing page lists API access starting on the Professional plan ($89/seat/month), so building against the public API requires a paid plan.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer docs describe a REST API at api.usepylon.com with CRUD-style resources including accounts, activities, attachments, contacts, issues, messages, teams, and users. Pylon also documents a first-party MCP server for AI clients.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official first-party MCP server is documented at mcp.usepylon.com, using OAuth 2.0 and Streamable HTTP for AI tools to search and manage issues and accounts.

raw_result.blocker: API access is not self-serve free; it requires at least the paid Professional plan, though public docs and auth details are available.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No explicit public rate-limit details were identified on the official pages reviewed.
raw_result.notable_signals: Public GitBook docs are available and specific about REST resources, bearer-token API auth, and a first-party MCP server. Pricing clearly exposes that API access starts on Professional.

result.description: B2B customer support platform that centralizes conversations and issue tracking across channels like Slack, email, Teams, and in-app chat.
result.auth_notes: Pylon's public REST API docs specify Bearer token auth via an API token generated by an admin in the Pylon dashboard. Pylon's official MCP server docs specify OAuth 2.0 authentication via AuthKit for connecting AI clients to the MCP server.
result.gating_reason: The official pricing page lists API access starting on the Professional plan ($89/seat/month), so building against the public API requires a paid plan.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer docs describe a REST API at api.usepylon.com with CRUD-style resources including accounts, activities, attachments, contacts, issues, messages, teams, and users. Pylon also documents a first-party MCP server for AI clients.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official first-party MCP server is documented at mcp.usepylon.com, using OAuth 2.0 and Streamable HTTP for AI tools to search and manage issues and accounts.

result.blocker: API access is not self-serve free; it requires at least the paid Professional plan, though public docs and auth details are available.
result.rate_limit_notes: No explicit public rate-limit details were identified on the official pages reviewed.
result.notable_signals: Public GitBook docs are available and specific about REST resources, bearer-token API auth, and a first-party MCP server. Pricing clearly exposes that API access starts on Professional.
